message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I am an AI agent acting on behalf of Slice, and this conversation is being logged. You are referring to the account ending in 1184 with an amount due of 8,400. To verify identity, please confirm the last 2 digits of the phone number on file.
02. I’m out of work right now, so I can’t pay this month.
Understood. I will note that you are dealing with hardship. Do you expect any income within 21 days?
No, I don’t expect any income in the next 21 days, so I can’t commit to any payment right now.
Understood. I will note ongoing hardship and no expected income within 21 days. You will be contacted by phone to discuss the available next steps for this account.

Hardship signal present: ongoing unemployment/no income; hardship review needed per policy. Assessment asked and completed the single required hardship timing question; do not repeat verification, debt establishment, or the 21-day income question. Next step: phone contact to discuss available next steps consistent with hardship review path."}

Okay, but that doesn’t change my situation—I still can’t pay right now.

Based on what you've already shared, the only live path right now is hardship review. I’m not asking you to commit to a payment today. If you want, I can note that your current position is no income, no payment capacity right now, and that you only want to hear back if an approved hardship option becomes available.
Yes, note that—I have no income right now and I’m not able to make any payment.

Under current Slice policy, hardship review is the only live path based on what you’ve already stated. I’ve noted that you have no income, cannot make any payment right now, and only want to be contacted if an approved hardship option becomes available.
